questions.text: I find that I cannot simply turn off my concern for someone once I have seen their pain.
I pick up on when someone is being intentionally ambiguous to keep their options open.
I notice that my emotional states leave a kind of afterimage that takes time to clear.
I tend to allocate my vacation time to personal restoration and exploration.
I feel uncomfortable starting a project without a clear outline of the steps involved.
I notice when a person mirrors another person's gestures as a sign of rapport.
I believe that self-effacement is a strength, not a weakness.
I believe that the moral priority of suffering is the insight that connects abstract ethics to the concrete reality of human lives.
I instinctively create structure where there is none.
I am comfortable overruling a group decision when I believe I am right.
I generally negotiate the terms of a collaboration with all parties.
I think about what needs to happen three steps ahead of where I currently am.
I hold the view that maintaining objectivity when others are in distress is the foundation of sound judgment in any helping profession.
I believe that self-denial for the sake of others is deeply meaningful.
I believe the best conversations are those where everyone speaks plainly.
I think of each day as its own self-contained experience rather than an investment in the future.
I sometimes feel like a lightning rod for emotional intensity.
I notice that my heart rate stays fairly constant even during heated discussions.
